overview: The design contains a single independent component with no dependencies, so all implementation occurs in a single foundational phase.

phases.name: Foundation Service
phases.description: The todo-api is the sole component and has no dependencies, so it is built in a single phase.

components.name: todo-api
responsibilities: Expose versioned RESTful CRUD endpoints for todo items with validation and standard error responses
Persist todos durably in a relational database with versioned migrations and indexed queries
Support list pagination, filtering, and sorting with sub-200ms performance
Provide liveness/readiness checks, structured request logging, and graceful shutdown
Enforce safe concurrent updates via optimistic concurrency (version column)
components.architectureContext: Self-contained HTTP service that clients call directly over JSON/HTTP on port 9090; it communicates downstream only with its PostgreSQL (or SQLite fallback) database. No other components depend on or are depended upon by this service.
keyConsiderations: Use parameterized queries and http.MaxBytesReader (1MB) to mitigate injection and oversized payloads
Implement optimistic concurrency using a version column and return 409 on If-Match mismatch
Ensure indexes on completed, priority, due_date, created_at to meet <200ms list latency at 100k rows
Handle malformed JSON (400 invalid_json) and unknown routes (404 not_found) in the standard error envelope
Graceful shutdown with 30s timeout on SIGINT/SIGTERM, closing DB connections cleanly
